name: njsparser

metadata.name: njsparser
metadata.summary: A Python NextJS data parser from HTML

documentation:
# NJSParser
A powerful **parser** and **explorer** for any website built with [NextJS](https://nextjs.org).
- Parses flight data (from the **`self.__next_f.push`** scripts).
- Parses next data from **`__NEXT_DATA__`** script.
- Parses **build manifests**.
- Searches for **build id**.
- Many other things ...

It uses only **lxml**, **orjson**, **pydantic** to garantee a fast and efficient data parsing and processing.
## Installation:
```
pip install njsparser
```
## Use
*TODO*

functions.name: njsparser.utils.make_tree
documentation:
Returns an lxml etree for the give str or bytes, and returns
the etree if the given argument is already one.

Args:
    value (_supported_tree): Str or bytes html page, or an etree.

Raises:
    TypeError: The tree isn't a string, bytes or etree.

Returns:
    etree._Element: The tree.

functions.name: njsparser.utils.join
documentation:
Joins the args to make an url path (for some reasons os.path.join
is shit for this and excludes some parts).

Returns:
    str: The url path.

functions.name: njsparser.tools.has_nextjs
documentation:
Tells if the page has some nextjs data in it.

Args:
    value (_supported_tree): The page to check for.

Returns:
    bool: True if it contains any nextjs data, otherwise, False.

functions.name: njsparser.tools.finditer_in_flight_data
documentation:
An iterator yielding flight data elements of specified types and matching a callback.

Args:
    flight_data (dict[int, FlightElement]): The flight data. Typically obtained using
        `njsparser.get_flight_data(...)`.
    class_filters (List[Type[T]], optional): A list of classes to filter the flight
        elements by type. If None, no type filtering is applied. Defaults to None.
    callback (Callable[[FlightElement], bool], optional): A function used to further filter
        elements. The function receives a single flight element as an argument and must
        return `True` to include the element in the results, or `False` to exclude it.
        For example, `lambda item: item.index >= 5` includes only elements with an `index`
        attribute greater than or equal to 5. If None, all elements are included. Defaults to None.

Yields:
    T: Flight elements matching the specified type and callback criteria.

functions.name: njsparser.tools.finditer_in_flight_data
documentation: See the main overload for `finditer_in_flight_data`.

functions.name: njsparser.tools.findall_in_flight_data
documentation:
A function returning all flight data elements of specified types and matching a callback.

Args:
    flight_data (dict[int, FlightElement]): The flight data, typically obtained using
        `njsparser.get_flight_data(...)`.
    class_filters (List[Type[T]], optional): A list of classes to filter the flight
        elements by type. If None, no type filtering is applied. Defaults to None.
    callback (Callable[[FlightElement], bool], optional): A function used to further filter
        elements. The function receives a single flight element as an argument and must
        return `True` to include the element in the results, or `False` to exclude it.
        For example, `lambda item: item.index >= 5` includes only elements with an `index`
        attribute greater than or equal to 5. If None, all elements are included. Defaults to None.

Returns:
    List[T]: A list of flight elements matching the specified type and callback criteria.

functions.name: njsparser.tools.findall_in_flight_data
documentation: See the main overload for `findall_in_flight_data`.

functions.name: njsparser.tools.find_in_flight_data
documentation:
Returns the first flight data element of specified types and matching a callback.

Args:
    flight_data (dict[int, FlightElement]): The flight data, typically obtained using
        `njsparser.get_flight_data(...)`.
    class_filters (List[Type[T]], optional): A list of classes to filter the flight
        elements by type. If None, no type filtering is applied. Defaults to None.
    callback (Callable[[FlightElement], bool], optional): A function used to further filter
        elements. The function receives a single flight element as an argument and must
        return `True` to include the element in the results, or `False` to exclude it.
        For example, `lambda item: item.index >= 5` includes only elements with an `index`
        attribute greater than or equal to 5. If None, all elements are included. Defaults to None.

Returns:
    T | None: The first flight element matching the specified type and callback criteria,
    or None if no match is found.

functions.name: njsparser.tools.find_in_flight_data
documentation: See the main overload for `find_in_flight_data`.

functions.name: njsparser.tools.find_build_id
documentation:
Searches and return (or not) the build id of the given page.

Args:
    value (_supported_tree): The page to find the build id from.

Returns:
    str | None: Either the buildId if it was found, or None if it didn't.

functions.name: njsparser.api.get_api_path
documentation:
Returns the path to the `index.json` file.

Args:
    build_id (str): The build id of the website.
    base_path (str, optional): The base path (can be obtained with
        `njsparser.tools.get_base_path(...)`). Defaults to None.
    path (str, optional): The path to the file to get. Defaults to
        `index.json`.

Returns:
    str | None: The url to the file, or None if the file has no api equivalent.

functions.name: njsparser.api.get_index_api_path
documentation:
Returns the path of the `index.json` api endpoint.

Args:
    build_id (str): The buildid of the website.
    base_path (str, optional): The base path (can be obtained with
        `njsparser.tools.get_base_path(...)`). Defaults to None.

Returns:
    str: The path to index.json endpoint.

functions.name: njsparser.api.is_api_exposed_from_response
documentation:
Tells if the api is exposed from the response of the request to
`f"https://{domain}{njsparser.utils.get_index_api_path(...)}"`.

Args:
    status_code (int): The status code of the response.
    content_type (str): The value of the `"Content-Type"` header from
        the response.
    text (str): The text content of the response.

Returns:
    bool: Is the api exposed ?
Works:
- https://coindrop.to/[piggybankName]
https://coindrop.to/_next/data/cSqeZiCyPxe_rbiYdsZdp/[piggybankName].json
Doesn't:
- https://www.bible.com/users/[username]
https://www.bible.com/_next/data/EhmmkHrUA0ygbv7dJJTtH/users/[username].json

functions.name: njsparser.api.list_api_paths
documentation:
Lists the api paths based of off the build manifest.

Args:
    sorted_pages (list[str]): The value `["sortedPages"]` from the
        parsed build manifest.
    build_id (str): The build id of the website.
    base_path (str): The base path (can be obtained by using the
        `njsparser.parser.get_base_path(...)` function).
    is_api_exposed (bool, optional): Is the api exposed ? Should be
        the result of `njsparser.is_api_exposed_from_response(...)`.
        Defaults to True.

Returns:
    list[str]: The list of api paths exposed.

functions.name: njsparser.parser.next_data.get_next_data
documentation:
Returns the dict content of the `<script id='__NEXT_DATA__'>`, if it exists.

Args:
    value (_supported_tree): The page to get the value from.

Returns:
    dict[str, Any] | None: The dict content of the script, if there is, otherwise
        None.

functions.name: njsparser.parser.next_data.has_next_data
documentation:
Tells if the given page contains a `<script id='__NEXT_DATA__'>`.

Args:
    value (_supported_tree): The page to check for.

Returns:
    bool: True if it contain any `__NEXT_DATA__` script, otherwise, False.

modules.name: njsparser.parser.flight_data
documentation: Part of the lib to interract with the nextjs data located looking like `self.__next_f.push(1, "...")`

functions.name: njsparser.parser.flight_data.has_flight_data
documentation:
Tells if a given page contains any flight data.

Args:
    value (_supported_tree): The page to check for.

Returns:
    bool: True if the page contains any flight data.

functions.name: njsparser.parser.flight_data.get_raw_flight_data
documentation:
Will return the raw flight data, under the same format as the array shown when
doing `console.log(self.__next_f);` in the console of a website containing nextjs
flight data.

Args:
    value (_supported_tree): The page to get the data from.

Returns:
    _raw_f_data | None: The `self.__next_f` array, or None if nothing found.

functions.name: njsparser.parser.flight_data.decode_raw_flight_data
documentation:
Decodes the raw flight data the same way that the client would do it.

Args:
    raw_flight_data (_raw_f_data): The raw flight data, coming from the
        output of `get_raw_flight_data(...)`, representing the array
        `self.__next_f` from a page that would contain flight data.

Raises:
    KeyError: Unknown segment type.
    UnboundLocalError

Returns:
    List[str]: The chunks of flight data.
https://chatgpt.com/share/674e2a5c-6a34-8007-a1f7-510a74d89d26
https://github.com/vercel/next.js/blob/5405f66fc78d02cc30afd0284630d91f676c3f38/packages/next/src/client/app-index.tsx#L43-L80

functions.name: njsparser.parser.flight_data.get_flight_data
documentation:
Returns the flight data of the page (the data contained in `self.__next_f`).

Args:
    value (_supported_tree): The page to get the data from.

Returns:
    dict[int, Any] | None: The flight data, if it exists, otherwise, None.

functions.name: njsparser.parser.urls.get_next_static_urls
documentation:
Lists all the paths found on the page that contains a `/_next/static/`
part.

Args:
    value (_supported_tree): The page to list the paths from.

Returns:
    list[str] | None: The list of paths, or None if none are found.

functions.name: njsparser.parser.urls.get_base_path
documentation:
Returns the base path of the `/_next/static/` paths. As an example:

If your page doesn't contain any base path to it, the paths will look
like `'/_next/static/chunks/blabla.js'`, and the full url will look like
`'https://www.your.site/_next/static/chunks/blabla.js'`. This function
would return `''` for this case.

But if it contains a base path, it might look something like
`'/hello/_next/static/chunks/blabla.js'`. The prefix would be `'/hello'`
in this case. The full url would look like
`'https://www.your.site/hello/_next/static/chunks/blabla.js'`. The
function will return `/hello` in this case.

If the paths are absolutes (already containing the domain, like on
`'https://example.com/hello/_next/static/blabla.png'`), you can set the
`remove_domain` argument to `True` to remove it, and get only the base
path. Will return `'/hello'` instead of `'https://example.com/hello'`.

Args:
    value (_supported_tree | list[str]): The page you want to get the
        nextjs paths prefixes from, or directly the list of paths you
        found.
    remove_domain (bool, optional): Removes the domain at the base of
        the url if there is(`'https://www.hello.com/hi'` -> `'/hi'`).
        Default to False.

Raises:
    AssertionError: One of the path doesn't contain the `/_next/static/`
        nextjs static path.
    AssertionError: One of the found start of the prefix is not matching
        the ones previously found.

Returns:
    str | None: Will return the string path, or None if the value is
        a page we didn't find any static nextjs path in it.

functions.name: njsparser.parser.manifests.parse_buildmanifest
documentation:
Parses the buildmanifest script (`"/_buildManifest.js"`).

Args:
    script (str): The content of the script.

Returns:
    dict[str, Any] | None: The content of the script, or nothing if no matches.

functions.name: njsparser.parser.manifests.get_build_manifest_path
documentation:
Gives the path of the build manifest based on the given build id
and base path.

Args:
    build_id (str): The build id of the website.
    base_path (str, optional): The base path (can be obtained with
        `njsparser.tools.get_base_path(...)`). Defaults to None.

Returns:
    str: The path of the build manifest.

functions.name: njsparser.parser.js.loads
documentation:
Parses a json-like coming from javascript.

Args:
    src (str): The source string to parse.
    kwargs (dict, optional): A dictionary of arguments for variable substitution.
    raise_on_excess_data (bool, optional): Do we raise if we found more data after
        finished the parsing ?

Returns:
    Any: A json data.

classes.name: njsparser.parser.js._Undefined
documentation: Represents a JS `undefined` value.

classes.name: njsparser.parser.js.Parser

methods.name: njsparser.parser.js.Parser.eof
documentation:
Checks if the parser has reached the end of the source string.

Returns:
    bool: True if end of file is reached, False otherwise.

methods.name: njsparser.parser.js.Parser.peek_one
documentation:
Returns the next character without consuming it.

Raises:
    ValueError: If the end of input is reached.

Returns:
    str: The next character.

methods.name: njsparser.parser.js.Parser.consume_one
documentation:
Consumes the next character and advances the index.

Raises:
    ValueError: If the end of input is reached.

Returns:
    str: The consumed character.

methods.name: njsparser.parser.js.Parser.consume_n
documentation:
Consumes the next `n` characters and advances the index.

Args:
    n (int): Number of characters to consume.

Raises:
    ValueError: If the end of input is reached before consuming `n` characters.

Returns:
    str: The consumed substring.

methods.name: njsparser.parser.js.Parser.test
documentation:
Checks if the upcoming characters match the given string.

Args:
    what (str): The string to test.

Returns:
    bool: True if the string matches, False otherwise.

methods.name: njsparser.parser.js.Parser.consume
documentation:
Consumes the given string if it matches the input.

Args:
    what (str): The string to consume.

Raises:
    ValueError: If the string does not match.

Returns:
    str: The consumed string.

methods.name: njsparser.parser.js.Parser.try_consume
documentation:
Tries to consume the given string if it matches the input.

Args:
    what (str): The string to consume.

Returns:
    Optional[str]: The consumed string if successful, otherwise None.

methods.name: njsparser.parser.js.Parser.skip_spaces
documentation: Skips whitespace characters in the input.

methods.name: njsparser.parser.js.Parser.value
documentation:
Parses a value from the input.

Raises:
    ValueError: If an unexpected character is encountered.

Returns:
    Any: The parsed value.

methods.name: njsparser.parser.js.Parser.object_key
documentation:
Parses an object key.

Returns:
    str: The parsed object key.

methods.name: njsparser.parser.js.Parser.object
documentation:
Parses an object from the input.

Returns:
    dict: The parsed object.

methods.name: njsparser.parser.js.Parser.array
documentation:
Parses an array or tuple from the input.

Args:
    opener (str): The opening bracket or parenthesis.
    closer (str): The closing bracket or parenthesis.

Returns:
    list: The parsed array or tuple.

methods.name: njsparser.parser.js.Parser.string
documentation:
Parses a string value.

Returns:
    str: The parsed string.

methods.name: njsparser.parser.js.Parser.var_name
documentation:
Parses a variable name.

Returns:
    str: The parsed variable name.

methods.name: njsparser.parser.js.Parser.number
documentation:
Parses a number.

Returns:
    float: The parsed number.

Raises:
    ValueError: If no valid number is found.

methods.name: njsparser.parser.js.Parser.loads
documentation:
Parses the entire source string as a JSON-like document.

Args:
    raise_on_excess_data (bool, optional): Do we raise if we found more data after
        finished the parsing ?

Returns:
    Any: The parsed value.

Raises:
    ValueError: If excess data is found after parsing.

A JSON-like parser that supports `undefined` and `void 0` values,
variable substitutions, and unquoted object keys.

Args:
    src (str): The source string to parse.
    kwargs (dict, optional): A dictionary of arguments for variable substitution.

functions.name: njsparser.cli.analyze
documentation:
Scan the given website to find any interesting exploitable data.

Args:
    url (str): The url of the website to analyze (can contain a path).

modules.name: njsparser.source
documentation: In buildmanifest:
url: https://core.voluti.com.br
"/accounting/balanceLedger/view": ["static/chunks/pages/accounting/balanceLedger/view-225856594a4838e7.js"]
Turns to:
https://core.voluti.com.br/_next/static/chunks/pages/accounting/balanceLedger/view-225856594a4838e7.js
Source is:
https://core.voluti.com.br/_next/static/chunks/pages/accounting/balanceLedger/view-225856594a4838e7.js.map
from https://github.com/0xM1gu3l/NextJSManifestDumper

functions.name: njsparser.parser.types.is_flight_data_obj
documentation:
Tells if the given object is a flight data item.

Args:
    value (Any): The object.

Returns:
    bool: True if it is, False if not.

functions.name: njsparser.parser.types.resolve_type
documentation:
Find the appropriate dataclass object to init the given value.

Args:
    value (Any): The value of the flight data item.
    value_class (str | None): The class of the flight data item.
    index (int): The index the flight data.

Returns:
    FlightElement: The appropriate element.

classes.name: njsparser.parser.types.FlightHintPreload

methods.name: njsparser.parser.types.FlightHintPreload.href
documentation:
The href of the object.

Returns:
    str: The href the link points to.

methods.name: njsparser.parser.types.FlightHintPreload.type_name
documentation:
The type name of the object.

Returns:
    str: The type name (font, style, ...)

methods.name: njsparser.parser.types.FlightHintPreload.attrs
documentation:
The additional attributes of the object.

Returns:
    None | dict[str, str]: The dict of it, or None if not attrs.

Represents a `"HL"` object. It is used to place some `<link>` tags into
the head of the document. Here are some examples of values:

`` `python
\>>> hl1 = FlightHintPreload(
...     value=[
...         "/_next/static/media/93f479601ee12b01-s.p.woff2",
...         "font",
...         {"crossOrigin": "", "type": "font/woff2"}
...     ],
...     value_class="HL",
... )
\>>> hl1.href
'/_next/static/media/93f479601ee12b01-s.p.woff2'
\>>> hl1.type_name
'font'
\>>> hl1.attrs
{"crossOrigin": "", "type": "font/woff2"}
\>>>
\>>> hl2 = FlightHintPreload(
...     value=[
...         "/_next/static/css/baf446a5c20b5fd4.css?dpl=dpl_F2qLi1zuzNsnuiFMqRXyYU9dbJYw",
...         "style"
...     ],
...     value_class="HL",
... )
\>>> hl2.href
'/_next/static/css/baf446a5c20b5fd4.css?dpl=dpl_F2qLi1zuzNsnuiFMqRXyYU9dbJYw'
\>>> hl2.type_name
'style'
\>>> hl2.attrs
\>>>
` ``

classes.name: njsparser.parser.types.FlightModule

methods.name: njsparser.parser.types.FlightModule.module_id
documentation:
Returns the module id.

Returns:
    int: The module id.

methods.name: njsparser.parser.types.FlightModule.module_scripts_raw
documentation:
Returns the raw script[script id: script relative path].

Returns:
    dict[str, str]: The script map.

methods.name: njsparser.parser.types.FlightModule.module_scripts
documentation:
The modules scripts id to their absolute path.

Returns:
    dict[str, str]: The script map with absolute paths.

methods.name: njsparser.parser.types.FlightModule.module_name
documentation:
The name of the module.

Returns:
    str: The name of the module.

Represents a `"I"` object. It is used to import some modules (list of
scripts). Here is an usage example:

`` `python
\>>> i = FlightModule(
...     value=[
...         30777,
...         [
...             "71523",
...             "static/chunks/25c8a87d-0d1c991f726a4cc1.js",
...             "10411",
...             "static/chunks/app/(webapp)/%5Blang%5D/(public)/user/layout-bd7c1d222b477529.js"
...         ],
...         "default"
...     ],
...     value_class="I",
... )
\>>> i.module_id
30777
\>>> i.module_scripts_raw()
{'71523': 'static/chunks/25c8a87d-0d1c991f726a4cc1.js', '10411': 'static/chunks/app/(webapp)/%5Blang%5D/(public)/user/layout-bd7c1d222b477529.js'}
\>>> i.module_scripts
{'71523': '/_next/static/chunks/25c8a87d-0d1c991f726a4cc1.js', '10411': '/_next/static/chunks/app/(webapp)/%5Blang%5D/(public)/user/layout-bd7c1d222b477529.js'}
\>>> i.module_name
'default'
` ``

classes.name: njsparser.parser.types.FlightText

methods.name: njsparser.parser.types.FlightText.text
documentation:
Returns the text content.

Returns:
    str: The text content.

Represents a `"T"` flight element. It simply contains text.

`` `python
\>>> t = FlightText(
...     value="hello world",
...     value_class="T"
... )
\>>> t.value
'hello world'
\>>> t.text
'hello world'
` ``

classes.name: njsparser.parser.types.FlightData
documentation:
Represents data in the flight content. It will only be used if the value
is None. Otherwise it's only here for inherithence of other data objects of
`value_class` `None`.

`` `python
\>>> fd = FlightData(
...     value=None,
...     value_class=None,
... )
\>>> fd.value
\>>> print(fd.value)
None
` ``

classes.name: njsparser.parser.types.FlightEmptyData
documentation: Represents flight data that is empty (`None`).

classes.name: njsparser.parser.types.FlightSpecialData
documentation:
Represents any special data in the page. It looks like a string starting
with a `"$"` character, such as `"$Sreact.suspense"`.

`` `python
\>>> fsd = FlightSpecialData(value="$Sreact.suspense", value_class=None)
\>>> fsd.value
'$Sreact.suspense'
` ``

classes.name: njsparser.parser.types.FlightHTMLElement

methods.name: njsparser.parser.types.FlightHTMLElement.tag
documentation:
Returns the tag of the html element, like "div", "link", ...

Returns:
    str: The tag.

methods.name: njsparser.parser.types.FlightHTMLElement.href
documentation:
The href, if there is one.

Returns:
    str | None: The str href or None.

methods.name: njsparser.parser.types.FlightHTMLElement.attrs
documentation:
The attributes.

Returns:
    dict[str, str]: The attributes.

Represents a `None` object containing HTML. It is used to store HTML. Here
is an example:

`` `python
\>>> h = FlightHTMLElement(
...     value=[
...         "$",
...         "link",
...         "https://sentry.io",
...         {
...             "rel": "dns-prefetch",
...             "href": "https://sentry.io"
...         }
...     ],
...     value_class=None,
... )
\>>> h.tag
'link'
\>>> h.href
'https://sentry.io'
\>>> h.attrs
{'rel': 'dns-prefetch', 'href': 'https://sentry.io'}
` ``

classes.name: njsparser.parser.types.FlightDataContainer
documentation:
Represents a list of `FlightData`. As seen on the example lower, you
will have to instanciate them yourself if you want to treat each one of
them as a FlightData object (see how `item` is done).

py
\>>> fdc = FlightDataContainer(
... value=\[
... ["\$", "div", None, {}],
... \["\$", "link", "<https://sentry.io>", {"rel": "dns-prefetch", "href": "<https://sentry.io>"}
... \]\],
... value_class=None
... )
\>>> fdc.value
\[["\$", "div", None, {}], \["\$", "link", "<https://sentry.io>", {"rel": "dns-prefetch", "href": "<https://sentry.io>"}\]\]
\>>> item = FlightData(value=fdc.value[0], value_class=fdc.value_class)
\>>> item.value
["\$", "div", None, {}]
\>>> item.content
{}

classes.name: njsparser.parser.types.FlightURLQuery
documentation:
Represents the values to be set in a url. Example:

`` `python
\>>> phv = FlightURLQuery(
...     value=[
...         "userId",
...         "624dc255c12744f2fdaf90c8",
...         "d"
...     ],
...     value_class=None,
... )
\>>> phv.key
'userId'
\>>> phv.val
'624dc255c12744f2fdaf90c8'
` ``

classes.name: njsparser.parser.types.FlightRSCPayloadVersion
documentation:
The RSCPayloadVersion. I have no idea what are the real names or version
codes, so i will just name them as old and new, will add more if find.

classes.name: njsparser.parser.types.FlightRSCPayload

methods.name: njsparser.parser.types.FlightRSCPayload.build_id
documentation:
Gives the build id from the rscpayload.

Returns:
    str: The build id.

Represents the RCSPayload, which is the payload containing infos about the
page. It contains a tree that, read by nextjs and react, will load the whole
page correctly.

https://github.com/vercel/next.js/blob/965fe24d91d08567751339756e51f2cf9d0e3188/packages/next/src/server/app-render/types.ts#L224-L243

classes.name: njsparser.parser.types.FlightError

methods.name: njsparser.parser.types.FlightError.digest
documentation:
The error code (digest).

Returns:
    str: The error code.

Represents a `"E"` (error) flight element. It contains an error.

`` `python
\>>> fe = FlightText(
...     value={"digest": "NEXT_NOT_FOUND"},
...     value_class="E"
... )
\>>> fe.digest
'NEXT_NOT_FOUND'
` ``
